questions.title: Validation scope
questions.context: KFTR-05 requires architecture validation — output must match cursor-based path. The cursor-based path handles keyed FROM/TO via SampleByFillPrevRecordCursor with Map iteration per bucket. Current fast-path code at lines 374-380 emits all-key fills for empty buckets. This may already work correctly for FROM/TO.

options.label: Spot-check comparison
options.description: Run the same keyed FROM/TO query on both paths (cursor vs fast), compare output. If identical, pass.

options.label: Exhaustive comparison
options.description: Run a suite of edge-case keyed FROM/TO queries (empty range, single bucket, DST crossing, keys appearing mid-range) on both paths. Compare all outputs.

options.label: Test-only validation
options.description: Write assertion tests with expected output derived from SQL semantics. Don't compare to cursor path directly.

questions.title: Structural changes needed?
questions.context: Current hasNext() at lines 365-388 handles dataTs > nextBucketTimestamp for keyed queries (empty bucket → emit all keys as fill). Leading fill works because nextBucketTimestamp starts at FROM, first data comes later. Trailing fill works because maxTimestamp=TO keeps the loop going after data exhaustion. The code may already be correct without changes.

options.label: Test first, fix if broken
options.description: Write the keyed FROM/TO tests first. If they pass without code changes, the phase is just tests + validation.

options.label: Review and harden first
options.description: Code-review the hasNext() keyed FROM/TO path before testing. Identify potential issues proactively.

questions.title: Keys appearing mid-range
questions.context: If key 'C' first appears at bucket 5 of 10, should buckets 1-4 have fill rows for 'C'? The two-pass design discovers all keys in pass 1, so 'C' is known before pass 2. But the fill row for 'C' at bucket 1 has no prev value yet (FILL(PREV) returns null until first data).

options.label: Fill all keys from FROM
options.description: All discovered keys get fill rows from the very first bucket. PREV starts as null until first data for that key. Matches cartesian product semantics.

options.label: Fill from first appearance
options.description: Keys only get fill rows from their first data appearance onward. Before that, no rows for that key. Matches cursor-path behavior.

questions.title: Empty range (FROM == TO)
questions.context: If FROM equals TO, the range [FROM, TO) is empty. Should the query return zero rows? The non-keyed case returns zero rows (maxTimestamp = TO, nextBucketTimestamp = FROM, FROM < TO is false → loop doesn't execute).

options.label: Zero rows
options.description: Empty range produces empty result set. Consistent with non-keyed behavior.

questions.title: Test scenarios
questions.context: The phase needs tests covering all KFTR requirements. The cursor-based path is the reference for expected behavior. Current SampleByFillTest has 15 tests (9 non-keyed, 6 keyed, none with FROM/TO + keys).

options.label: Minimal (3-4 tests)
options.description: Basic keyed FROM/TO NULL, VALUE, PREV + one edge case. Quick to implement.

options.label: Comprehensive (6-8 tests)
options.description: Cover all KFTR requirements: leading fill, trailing fill, PREV tracking, mid-range key appearance, empty range, DST + FROM/TO + keys.

questions.title: Cursor-path comparison tests
questions.context: For KFTR-05, should we add tests that explicitly compare fast-path output to cursor-path output for the same query? This requires running the query on both paths and asserting identical results. The cursor path is activated by skipping the SAMPLE BY rewrite (e.g., using ALIGN TO FIRST OBSERVATION instead of ALIGN TO CALENDAR).

options.label: No direct comparison
options.description: Test against hardcoded expected output only. The cursor path is the implicit reference but not executed in tests.

options.label: Side-by-side comparison
options.description: Run query on both paths, assert identical output. Stronger validation but requires a way to force cursor-path execution.
